column7: Apply after sprigs are planted but before emergence of bermudagrass or weeds
column19: Plant sprigs 2 inches deep in well-prepared seedbed. Do not treat areas where sprigs are planted less than 2 inches deep

column7: Apply after bermudagrass is planted but before germinating weeds reach 1 inch in height
column19: Will kill or injure legumes. Do not use on bentgrass carpetgrass buffalograss and St. Augustine grass

column7: Apply after bermudagrass is planted but before germinating weeds reach 1 inch in height
column19: Do not spray on legumes. Use NIS at 0.25 percent v/v

column7: Apply preemergence before weeds emerge
column19: Rainfall is necessary for herbicide activation into soil

column7: Apply soon after harvest or during dormancy several weeks prior to weed seed germination
column19: Apply only to established perennial warm season grasses. Do not exceed 5 fl oz per acre per application or 6 fl oz per year

column7: Apply to weeds 2 to 6 inches tall
column19: Apply low volatile esters October through March. Apply only nonvolatile amine forms April through late June. Do not spray on legumes such as clovers lespedeza or alfalfa

column7: Apply November or December when winter annuals emerge and repeat in late February or March
column19: Apply when temperature is 65F or above. Do not spray on legumes such as clovers lespedeza or alfalfa

column7: Apply when weeds are smaller than 4 inches tall
column19: Can be used on all pasture grasses. Do not make applications less than 7 days apart. Do not apply more than 3 applications per season

column7: Apply at 2.0 oz per acre to provide control of most pasture weeds when applied early in the season
column19: Hay treated with Chaparral in the preceding 18 months CANNOT be distributed off the farm. Use a reduced rate of NIS on tall fescue

column6: Part A 0.25-1 oz plus Part B 1-4 pt
column7: Apply to actively growing weeds
column19: Will control Pensacola bahiagrass. Will injure tall fescue with Part A rates above 0.25 oz

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing. Apply to perennial weeds when larger later in the season
column19: Do not use more than 32 fl oz per acre per season. Will kill clovers alfalfa and other legumes

column7: Apply when weeds are actively growing. Best time to treat biennial and winter annual weeds is when the plant is in rosette stage
column19: May not be applied to forage that is to be cut and sold for commercial purposes. Do not allow lactating dairy animals to graze treated areas until the next growing season

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: Will kill clovers alfalfa and other legumes. Do not apply to newly seeded grass

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: Residue in hay and manure can cause off-site damage to sensitive plants

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: Apply with COC at 1 percent v/v or MSO at 1 to 2 pints per acre

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing. Apply to perennial weeds during rosette stage
column19: Residue in hay and manure can cause off-site problems. May increase the palatability of poisonous plants

column7: Apply during warm weather when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: Apply to established grass forage. Do not apply more than 8.42 pt per acre per season

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Apply no more than 10 pints per acre per year

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Do NOT use treated grass or manure from animals being fed treated grass for composting

column7: Apply after bermudagrass has reached 100 percent green-up
column19: Only apply on bermudagrass. Some varieties including Jiggs are more sensitive. Do not use on World Feeder bermudagrass

column7: Apply to control broadleaf weeds prior to grass planting or after grasses are well established
column19: DO NOT apply in area of desirable legumes. DO NOT use treated hay or manure off-site

column7: Postemergence while weeds are actively growing. Apply to brush after full leaf expansion
column19: Hay treated within preceding 18 months may only be used on the farm where product was applied. Legumes such as clover and alfalfa are highly sensitive

column7: Best suited for cool season broadleaf weeds. Apply when weeds are actively growing
column19: Used to control broadleaf weeds in grass pastures mixed with established perennial white clover and lespedeza. White clover and lespedeza will exhibit some injury but will recover. Will kill red clover

column7: Apply when johnsongrass is actively growing and is at least 18 to 24 inches tall up to heading stage
column19: May be used on bermudagrass and bahiagrass. For best weed control do not mow or harvest the pasture for 2 weeks before or 2 weeks after application

column7: Apply within 7 days of hay cutting when broadleaf weeds are less than 4 inches tall and grasses are less than 2 inches tall
column19: Labeled in bermudagrass only. Do not make more than 2 applications per year. Allow at least 16 days between applications

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing
column19: Do NOT use on legumes. Do not allow lactating dairy animals to graze treated areas until the next growing season

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing and to woody vegetation when plants are in full leaf
column19: Do not allow lactating dairy animals to graze treated areas until the next growing season

column7: Apply to young actively growing annual weeds while perennial weeds are in bud to bloom or when biennials are in fall rosette
column19: Do not exceed 1.3 oz per acre per season. Desirable broadleaf crops like clover and alfalfa are sensitive and severe injury may occur. Rates higher than 0.5 oz per acre may injure fescue

column7: Apply immediately after first bermudagrass hay cutting before new growth has started
column19: Broadcast application during growing season may cause growth stunting and yellowing. Spot treatment should not exceed 10 percent of the total pasture

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing. Apply as soon as possible after the removal of hay
column19: Do not apply more than 2 applications or exceed 1.33 oz per acre in a 12-month period

column7: Apply when weeds are small and actively growing
column19: In-season applications will damage bahiagrass. Dormant-season applications are allowed for burndown

column7: Apply to annual weeds when small and actively growing and to woody vegetation when plants are in full leaf
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Do NOT use treated grass or manure from animals being fed treated grass for composting

column7: Apply one time per year when weeds are actively growing
column19: Labeled in bermudagrass and bahiagrass only. Treated forage grasses may not be fed for hay until 38 days after application

column7: Apply one time per year when weeds are actively growing
column19: Labeled in bermudagrass and bahiagrass only. Liquid form of Velpar DF

column7: Apply after bermudagrass is planted but before germinating weeds reach 1 inch in height
column19: Do not spray on legumes. Use NIS at 0.25 percent v/v

column7: Apply when there is no green tissue at the root crown or on stolons
column19: Add 10 to 20 gallons per acre of liquid nitrogen as the spray carrier

column7: Apply targeting winter and early season weeds during dormancy
column19: Labeled in bermudagrass only. Do not make more than 2 applications per year

column7: Apply to actively growing weeds. For bahiagrass control use 0.3 oz after green-up and before seedhead formation
column19: Will damage fescue at rates above 0.4 oz. Following applications at 0.1-0.3 oz alfalfa clovers bermudagrass ryegrass and tall fescue may be planted after 4 months

column7: Apply when bermudagrass is dormant
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. NIS at 0.25 percent v/v or COC at 0.5 percent v/v required

column7: Apply to actively growing winter broadleaf weeds and grasses along with weedy infestations of dallisgrass
column19: Spot treatment should not exceed 10 percent of the total pasture. Significant injury may occur to forage grass with green living tissue present

column7: Apply over the top when weeds are small and when sorghum plants are 4 to 6 inches tall
column19: DO NOT spray over-the-top when sorghum plants are less than 4 inches tall or more than 10 inches tall

column7: Apply after sorghum has emerged but before weeds are 1.5 inches tall
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Do Not use on sandy loams or coarser soil textures

column7: Apply after sorghum has emerged but before weeds are 1.5 inches tall
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Do Not use on sandy loams or coarser soil textures

column7: Apply in split-applications 10-21 days apart when fescue is actively growing and no more than 4 inches high
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Controls endophyte-infected fescue. Control will be better if fescue is not allowed to seed the previous year

column7: Apply to actively growing tall fescue after it has 4 to 6 inches of new growth and before the seedhead has emerged
column19: DO NOT mow tall fescue for 30 days before or 14 days after treatment. For greater suppression apply up to 2.5 pt per acre

column7: Apply with 1 qt followed by 1 qt after regrowth occurs again
column19: Endophyte infected fescue as well as other grasses. Do not allow plants to reproduce seed

column7: Apply when bermudagrass is dormant. For little barley control apply before the mid-boot stage
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. NIS at 0.25 percent v/v or COC at 0.5 percent v/v required

column7: Apply when grain is in full tiller stage but before boot stage
column19: Limited to one POST application per cycle. Be aware of drift concerns and laws in your area affecting 2,4-D

column7: Apply and incorporate just before planting
column19: Can be applied on alfalfa birdsfoot trefoil lespedeza. DO NOT use on white dutch clover. Established stands of alfalfa ladino clover can be sprayed 2.5-3.5 pt prior to weed emergence

column7: Apply on established plantings during the dormant season during late fall or winter months after the last fall cutting and before first spring cutting
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. Apply only during dormant season

column7: Apply during the fall or winter months. For best control apply under cool temperature conditions 55-60F
column19: Restricted Use Pesticide. May be applied both PRE and POST. PHI for grazing and haying is 120 days. PHI for harvest seed is 50 days

column7: Apply when alfalfa or clover is in second trifoliate stage or larger and when weeds are 1-3 inches tall
column19: Labeled for use on alfalfa birdsfoot trefoil and clover. May be applied to seedling alfalfa or clover

column7: Apply to seedling or established alfalfa
column19: For repeat applications make on a minimum of a 14 day interval

column7: Apply before mowing for best control
column19: Annual grasses only. Always add a COC at 0.5 percent v/v
